name: Tape

unions.name: Direction
unions.comment:
The direction enum selects parts of the tape that shall be extracted/modified.

Each function might interpret each direction slightly differently. Check the
documentation for more details.

unions.name: How
unions.comment: Declare how to move in the tape.

values.name: advance
values.comment:
Advance tape to the last element

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> advance
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, b, c, d, => e ]"

values.name: all
values.comment:
Determine if all elements satisfy some test.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> all (\x -> String.length x < 2)
        --> True

values.name: any
values.comment:
Determine if any element satisfy some test.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> any (\x -> x == "e")
        --> True

values.name: create
values.comment:
Create a tape from the left, head, and right parts.

The tape is conceptually equivalent to the list `left ++ [head] ++ right`
and has a cursor that points to the tape head.

    create [ "a", "b" ] "c" [ "d", "e" ]
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, b, => c, d, e ]"
values.type: List.List a -> a -> List.List a -> Tape.Tape a

values.name: elemIndex
values.comment:
Return index of element in tape

Elements left of head have negative indexes.

values.name: filter
values.comment:
Filter the given selection of tape with predicate.

Filter never removes the head, which makes `filter Head` a no-op and
`filter DirectoinTail` and `filter Direction` equivalent.

If you want to filter the head, convert to a list before
values.type: Tape.Direction -> (a -> Basics.Bool) -> Tape.Tape a -> Tape.Tape a

values.name: foldl
values.comment:
Reduce a tape from the left.

It has the same effect as reducing the equivalent list.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> foldl (++) "$"
        --> "edcba$"
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> Tape.Tape a -> b

values.name: foldr
values.comment:
Reduce a tape from the right.

It has the same effect as reducing the equivalent list.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> foldr (++) "$"
        --> "abcde$"
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> Tape.Tape a -> b

values.name: fromList
values.comment:
Try to create tape from List. Empty lists return Nothing.

    (fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ]
        |> Maybe.map toList)
        --> Just [ 1, 2, 3 ]

values.name: fromListOrDefault
values.comment:
Create a tape from List or a singleton tape with the given default value.

    fromListOrDefault 0 [ 1, 2, 3 ]
        |> toList
        --> [ 1, 2, 3 ]

values.name: fromParts
values.comment:
Construct tape from left and right tails.

    fromParts (tail Left tape) (head tape) (tail Right tape) ==> tape
values.type: List.List a -> a -> List.List a -> Tape.Tape a

values.name: indexedMap
values.comment:
Same as map but the function is also applied to the index of each element.

The head has an index of zero and the left hand side of the tape is indexed with
negative numbers.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> indexedMap (\i x -> String.fromInt i ++ x)
        |> show identity
        --> "[ -2a, -1b, => 0c, 1d, 2e ]"
values.type: (Basics.Int -> a -> b) -> Tape.Tape a -> Tape.Tape b

values.name: intersperse
values.comment:
Places the given value between all members of the given tape.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> intersperse "x"
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, x, b, x, => c, x, d, x, e ]"

values.name: isEmptyLeft
values.comment: True if tape has no elements to the left o head

values.name: isEmptyRight
values.comment: True if tape has no elements to the right o head

values.name: left
values.comment:
Move at most n positions to the left.

Negative steps move to the right.

    create ["a", "b", "c"] "d" ["e"]
        |> left 2
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, => b, c, d, e ]"

values.name: leftWith
values.comment:
Move head to the left according to strategy

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> leftWith (Cycle 3)
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, b, c, d, => e ]"

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
         |> leftWith (Default ("?", 4))
         |> show identity
         --> "[ => ?, ?, a, b, c, d, e ]"

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
         |> leftWith (Condition ((/=) "b"))
         |> show identity
         --> "[ a, => b, c, d, e ]"

    create ["a", "b"] "cc" ["d", "e"]
        |> leftWith (Carry 2)
        |> show identity
        --> "[ => cc, a, b, d, e ]"
values.type: Tape.How a -> Tape.Tape a -> Tape.Tape a

values.name: length
values.comment:
Determine the length of a tape.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> length
        --> 5

values.name: map
values.comment:
Apply a function to every element of a tape.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> map String.toUpper
        |> show identity
        --> "[ A, B, => C, D, E ]"

values.name: map2
values.comment:
Combine two tapes with function.

It keeps the smallest left and right tails of each tape.

    (map2 (++)
        (create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"])
        (repeat 1 5 "x")
    )
        |> show identity
        --> "[ bx, => cx, dx, ex ]"
values.type: (a -> b -> result) -> Tape.Tape a -> Tape.Tape b -> Tape.Tape result

values.name: map3
values.comment: Generalize map for 3 tapes
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> result) -> Tape.Tape a -> Tape.Tape b -> Tape.Tape c -> Tape.Tape result

values.name: map4
values.comment: Generalize map for 4 tapes
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> result) -> Tape.Tape a -> Tape.Tape b -> Tape.Tape c -> Tape.Tape d -> Tape.Tape result

values.name: map5
values.comment: Generalize map for 5 tapes
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> result) -> Tape.Tape a -> Tape.Tape b -> Tape.Tape c -> Tape.Tape d -> Tape.Tape e -> Tape.Tape result

values.name: maximum
values.comment: Find the maximum element in a non-empty list.

values.name: member
values.comment:
Figure out whether a tape contains a value.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> member "b"
        --> True

values.name: minimum
values.comment: Find the minimum element in a non-empty list.

values.name: parts
values.comment:
Separate the list in the (left, head, tail) parts.

The parts can be assembled back together with `create left head tail`
values.type: Tape.Tape a -> ( List.List a, a, List.List a )

values.name: position
values.comment:
Determine the position of head.

Position corresponds to the number of steps we must move
right from a rewinded tape.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> position
        --> 2

values.name: positionalMap
values.comment:
Similar to indexedMap, but uses the position of each element, which is
equivalent to the index of a rewinded tape.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> positionalMap (\i x -> String.fromInt i ++ x)
        |> show identity
        --> "[ 0a, 1b, => 2c, 3d, 4e ]"
values.type: (Basics.Int -> a -> b) -> Tape.Tape a -> Tape.Tape b

values.name: product
values.comment: Get the product of the list elements.

values.name: pushLeft
values.comment:
Push element to the left of the current head and move the cursor to it.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> pushLeft "changed"
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, b, => changed, c, d, e ]"

values.name: pushRight
values.comment:
Push element to the right of the current head and move the cursor to it.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> pushRight "changed"
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, b, c, => changed, d, e ]"

values.name: range
values.comment:
Create a tape of numbers, every element increasing by one.

The arguments are the lowest and highest numbers that should be on the tape.

    range 0 5
        |> toList
        --> [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 ]

values.name: read
values.comment:
Read element on the current position.

    create [1, 2] 3 [4, 5]
        |> read
        --> 3

values.name: redo
values.comment:
An alias to `right 1` that is more idiomatic when using the tape as a history container.

    create ["a", "b", "c"] "d" ["e"]
        |> redo
        |> show identity
            --> "[ a, b, c, d, => e ]"

values.name: reduce
values.comment:
Reduce tape applying binary operator.

Similar to a left fold, but it does not require an initial accumulator.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> reduce (++)
        --> "abcde"

    create [] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> reduce (++)
        --> "cde"

values.name: repeat
values.comment:
Create a tape with n copies of a value and tries to place m of them
before the head.

`repeat 0` behaves similarly to List.repeat, creating a tape with n elements
pointing to the first element.

Empty negative n are ignored, returning a tape with a single element.

    repeat 2 5 "a"
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, a, => a, a, a ]"

values.name: reverse
values.comment:
Flip a tape around the head.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> reverse
        |> show identity
        --> "[ e, d, => c, b, a ]"

values.name: rewind
values.comment:
Rewind tape so the head points to the first element

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> rewind
        |> show identity
        --> "[ => a, b, c, d, e ]"

values.name: right
values.comment:
Move at most n positions to the right.

Negative values move to the left.

    create ["a", "b", "c"] "d" ["e"]
        |> right 2
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, b, c, d, => e ]"

values.name: rightWith
values.comment:
Move head to the right according to strategy

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> rightWith (Cycle 3)
        |> show identity
        --> "[ => a, b, c, d, e ]"

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
            |> rightWith (Default ("?", 4))
            |> show identity
            --> "[ a, b, c, d, e, ?, => ? ]"

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
            |> rightWith (Condition ((/=) "b"))
            |> show identity
            --> "[ a, b, c, d, => e ]"

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> rightWith (Carry 2)
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, b, d, e, => c ]"
values.type: Tape.How a -> Tape.Tape a -> Tape.Tape a

values.name: select
values.comment:
Select parts of a tape and return it as a list

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> select All
        --> [ "a", "b", "c", "d", "e" ]

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> select Left
        --> [ "a", "b", "c" ]

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> select Backwards
        --> [ "c", "b", "a" ]

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> select Head
        --> [ "c" ]

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> select Tails
        --> [ "a", "b", "d", "e" ]

values.name: show
values.comment: Show tape as string, with emphasis on head element

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Create a tape with only one element

values.name: sort
values.comment:
Sort values from lowest to highest

Head moves to keep track of current element.

    create ["d"] "c" ["a", "e", "b"]
        |> sort
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, b, => c, d, e ]"

values.name: sortBy
values.comment:
Sort values by a derived property.

Head moves to keep track of current element.

    create ["d"] "cc" ["a", "eee", "bb"]
        |> sortBy String.length
        |> show identity
        --> "[ d, a, => cc, bb, eee ]"

values.name: sortWith
values.comment:
Sort values with a custom comparison function.

Head moves to keep track of current element.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> Tape.Tape a -> Tape.Tape a

values.name: sum
values.comment: Get the sum of the list elements.

values.name: take
values.comment: Take elements from one specific direction on the tape
values.type: Tape.Direction -> Basics.Int -> Tape.Tape a -> Tape.Tape a

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Converts tape to list

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> toList
        --> ["a", "b", "c", "d", "e"]

values.name: tryLeft
values.comment:
Try to move to the left.

Return Nothing if the tape would overflow on the leftmost position.

    create ["a"] "b" ["c", "d", "e"]
        |> tryLeft 2
        --> Nothing

values.name: tryRight
values.comment:
Try to move to the right.

Return Nothing if the tape would overflow on the rightmost position.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d"]
        |> tryRight 2
        --> Nothing

values.name: undo
values.comment:
An alias to `left 1` that is more idiomatic when using the tape as a history container.

    create ["a", "b", "c"] "d" ["e"]
        |> undo
        |> show identity
            --> "[ a, b, => c, d, e ]"

values.name: unzip
values.comment:
Decompose a tape of tuples into a tuple of tapes.

    create [("a", 1), ("b", 2)] ("c", 3) [("d", 4), ("e", 5)]
        |> unzip
        |> Tuple.mapBoth (show identity) (show String.fromInt)
        --> ( "[ a, b, => c, d, e ]", "[ 1, 2, => 3, 4, 5 ]" )
values.type: Tape.Tape ( a, b ) -> ( Tape.Tape a, Tape.Tape b )

values.name: update
values.comment:
A partial map on the tape, preserving the types of elements.

We can specify a direction to update from the head

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> update Right String.toUpper
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, b, => C, D, E ]"

The direction can be one of the following:

    - `Left` - only the left part is modified, including the head.
    - `Right` - only the right part is modified, including the head.
    - `LeftTail` - only the left part is modified, excluding the head.
    - `RightTail` - only the right part is modified, excluding the head.
    - `Backwards` - same as Left.
    - `BackwardsTail` - same as LeftTail.
    - `Head` - only the head is modified.
    - `Tails` - modify everything exept the head.
    - `InsideOut` - like All.
    - `All` - all elements are modified.
values.type: Tape.Direction -> (a -> a) -> Tape.Tape a -> Tape.Tape a

values.name: write
values.comment:
Write element to the tape's current position.

    create ["a", "b"] "c" ["d", "e"]
        |> write "changed"
        |> show identity
        --> "[ a, b, => changed, d, e ]"
